title: Create initial error types and config loading foundation
description: Implement the core error enum DaemonError and the TOML config parsing/validation layer needed before other modules can be built. Deliverables: src/errors.rs with JSON-RPC error-code mapping, src/config.rs with DaemonConfig/BotConfig/SigningConfig structs, env-var and path expansion, duplicate bot_id validation, config file permission checks, and unit tests. Covers R7, R8, and U1.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test passes for config and error modules; invalid config cases (duplicate bot_id, loose permissions, missing fields) return clear errors

title: Set up schema-first contract artifacts and codegen
description: Establish the schemas/ directory as the canonical machine-readable contract and wire the cargo xtask codegen runner. Deliverables: schemas/config.json, schemas/jsonrpc.json, schemas/metrics.json, schemas/service-compatibility.json, xtask/src/main.rs with a codegen command, generated Rust type stubs, and tests/schema_sync.rs that fails when schemas and generated code diverge. This satisfies R32 and U13.
acceptance_criteria: schemas/ contains four JSON files; cargo xtask codegen runs and writes generated types; cargo test includes schema_sync.rs

title: Initialize and scaffold pacto-bot-api Rust project
description: Create the foundational Rust crate structure for the pacto-bot-api daemon. This is greenfield work: no src/, Cargo.toml, or build files exist yet. Deliverables: Cargo.toml with binary targets (pacto-bot-api and pacto-bot-admin), src/ module tree (main.rs, admin.rs, config.rs, errors.rs, etc.), basic CLI argument parsing with clap, initial TOML config loader with serde, example config file, and a passing cargo check. This unblocks all subsequent implementation units U1–U15.
acceptance_criteria: cargo check passes; both binary targets resolve; src/ module tree matches plan section 'Crate Structure'; example config exists and parses

title: Configure Rust linting and security tooling
description: Add linting and audit configuration that enforces the security posture from the plan, especially secret hygiene. Deliverables: clippy.toml with custom lints forbidding plain String for secrets, deny.toml for cargo-deny audit gates, and initial CI-friendly verification commands. This supports R34 and U15.
acceptance_criteria: cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes on scaffolded code; cargo deny check runs without configuration errors
